title: Your Quiet Helpers
summary: How innate-immunity cells calmly check, learn from, and clear pathogens — framed as protection, not war.

questions.prompt: Macrophages are immune cells that work mostly by…
choices: Surrounding and engulfing microbes the body decides to clear
questions.explanation: Macrophages are big patrol cells — they engulf microbes and debris through a process called phagocytosis.

questions.prompt: Innate immunity is best described as the body's…
choices: First, quick, general response that you're born with
Slow custom response that learns over months
Way to make new red blood cells
questions.explanation: Innate immunity is the fast, general first response — you have it from birth, and it doesn't need to learn each microbe individually.

questions.prompt: Skin is part of your immune defense because it…
choices: Forms a calm physical barrier most microbes can't cross
questions.explanation: Skin is the body's first barrier — most microbes never get past intact skin.

questions.prompt: Most encounters between your immune cells and microbes end with…
choices: The immune cell deciding the microbe is safe and letting it pass
questions.explanation: Most microbes the immune system meets are harmless — it learns to wave them through. Only a small fraction trigger a full response.

questions.prompt: Washing your hands helps your immune system because it…
choices: Removes microbes before they ever reach the body's barriers
questions.explanation: Hand-washing physically removes microbes from your hands so they never get a chance to enter your eyes, nose, or mouth.

questions.prompt: Inflammation around a small cut usually means…
choices: Immune cells are arriving to clean up and help repair
questions.explanation: Redness, warmth, and a little swelling are signs that blood flow and immune cells are flowing to the area to clean up and help repair.
